content.full_text:
March 9, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807  

ATTENTION: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen  

RE: File No. A-90-109  
The Doctors' Company, The Doctors' Management Company, Gordon Cologne and Associates  

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I have your letter of March 6, 1990, and will, of course, act accordingly.

I would like you to know the conclusion you reached in this letter contradicts the oral instructions I received from two different people in the F.P.P.C. Technical Assistance Division. I say this only for the purpose of putting on record the fact that a good faith effort was made to find the correct way to handle the technicality of this reporting over the last two years and to abide by the instructions I was given.

You will note there has a full disclosure of all payments made and received although it was done by my identifying the principal as The Doctors' Company while the employer reports were made by the attorney-in-fact, The Doctors' Management Company.

It is important that the record show our good faith efforts in this matter.

In my conversation with Mr. Braaten-Moen since receiving your letter, I was assured of two additional points which I would also like to have as a matter of record.

I represent two other insurance exchanges -- Southern California Physicians Insurance Exchange and Medical Insurance Exchange of California -- who operate much like The Doctors' Company using an attorney-in-fact for their management. These firms, however, report their expenses for my lobbying activities in the name of the principal (the not-for-profit insurance company), and I report the income from the principal even though the payment is received from the management company. Mr. Braaten-Moen tells me that this is all right even though it is different from the way you suggest here because the name I use in reporting income coincides with the name of the employer making the employer report. If you have any different understanding I would like to hear from your office so that I may advise those clients of the best way to treat these reports.

Secondly, I lobby the insurance issues for these clients and represent to the legislators that I lobby for the "not-for-profit physician-owned insurance companies", naming the principal, in this case The Doctors' Company. Can I make this representation if the Secretary of State does not have on file the authorization from the principal, here The Doctors' Company, but has only an authorization from the attorney-in-fact, here The Doctors' Management Company? This appears to me to raise an ethical problem so I would like confirmation. I was assured by Mr. Braaten-Moen that it was all right since the authorization I had was from the attorney-in-fact. If your opinion is any different, I would like a response from you as it is our desire to comply with the spirit as well as the letter of the law.

Yours very truly,

Gordon Cologne

cc: Secretary of State,
    Attention: Brenda Richards

JC3.9.90
